title: radreport: A rule-based Python library for parsing, de-identifying, and structuring radiology free-text reports to FHIR
description:
radreport is a zero-dependency Python library and CLI that converts unstructured radiology free-text reports into structured, machine-readable data. It is designed for EMRs, telehealth platforms, billing systems, and research/analytics pipelines that need clean structured data from raw dictated or transcribed report text — without relying on ML models, GPUs, or external services.

What it does:

  1. Parse — splits any free-text radiology report into labeled sections (Indication, Technique, Findings, Impression, etc.), extracts measurements, and links findings to anatomical structures.
  2. Detect — flags critical/urgent findings (e.g. pulmonary embolism, pneumothorax, hemorrhage) with negation- and sentence-boundary-aware logic, so “no pneumothorax” is never mistaken for a positive finding.
  3. De-identify — redacts PHI (dates, ages 90+, SSNs, MRNs, accession numbers, phone/fax numbers, emails, URLs, IP addresses, ZIP codes, and titled/labeled names) with a full, offset-based audit trail, so reports can safely leave a controlled environment for research or analytics use.
  4. Export — outputs FHIR R4 DiagnosticReport resources ready to load into any EMR or health information system.

Why it exists: Radiology reports are produced as free text (often via PDF or dictation transcripts), but downstream systems need structured data. Existing solutions typically require ML pipelines, cloud NLP services, or GPUs — impractical for many hospital IT environments with strict data-residency and vendor-approval constraints. radreport is entirely rule-based, has zero required dependencies, and runs anywhere Python 3.9+ runs, making it straightforward to pass hospital IT and security review.

Key features: section parsing with mid-sentence-safe header detection; measurement extraction linked to anatomy; critical/urgent findings detection with negation awareness; follow-up imaging recommendation extraction; PHI de-identification with reproducible, reviewable audit trails; FHIR R4 export; a CLI for single-file and batch processing with JSON and flat CSV output; 90+ unit tests; MIT licensed.

Install: pip install radreport

Source code: https://github.com/mustafamm072/radreport — PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/radreport/
